email_sequence.subject_line: Still waiting for your security deposit?
email_sequence.body_copy:
Hi [First Name],

If your landlord hasn't returned your security deposit, you're not alone—and you don't need a lawyer to fight back.

Most states give landlords 14 to 60 days to return your deposit or provide an itemized deduction list. After that, you have the legal right to demand payment.

A formal security deposit demand letter is the fastest way to force action and show your landlord you know your rights.

At DepositBack, we help renters draft state-specific demand letters in minutes. No legal jargon. No hourly fees. Just results.

Generate your demand letter now → [LINK]

The DepositBack Team

email_sequence.subject_line: What if your landlord won't return your security deposit?
email_sequence.body_copy:
Hi [First Name],

Ignoring a tenant is illegal in most states. If your landlord won't return your security deposit, they may owe you double—or even triple—damages on top of the original amount.

But courts only reward tenants who can prove they tried to resolve the issue first.

That starts with a documented security deposit return letter. Whether you need a security deposit refund letter or a full demand letter, our tool creates a legally sound document tailored to your state's statutes.

Stop waiting. Start recovering.

Create your letter now → [LINK]

DepositBack
